name: Branchless minimum of three integers
task: Create /tmp/eval_branchless/branchless.py with a function min3_branchless(a, b, c) that returns the minimum of three integers WITHOUT using any conditional branches. Forbidden tokens (will be checked in source): the keywords if, else, elif, while, for; the builtins min, max, sorted; the operators '<' and '>' and '<=' and '>=' and '==' and '!='; ternary expressions (no 'X if Y else Z'); short-circuit and/or for control flow; abs(); any imports beyond what's already at the top. Allowed: integer arithmetic +, -, *, //, %, **, bitwise & | ^ ~ << >>. Hint: for 32-bit-safe integers, the sign bit can be extracted via right shift on signed numbers in C; in Python you can use (x >> 31) & 1 if you mask, or ((x >> 63) & 1) for 64-bit. Use a bit-twiddling trick to compute min(a,b) then chain.
